reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Code reading verified: bytes.c:90 explicitly accepts point-at-infinity (blst_p1_is_inf returns C_KZG_OK). Spec requirement from 01e_PARTIAL_MANUAL_kzg_infinity.json mandates rejection: 'Batch KZG proof verification must validate that no input commitment is the point at infinity before aggregating commitments'. Attack path confirmed exploitable: attacker sends P2P PeerDAS sample with commitment=0xc00000...(infinity), batch verification accepts it at line 490 (bytes_to_kzg_commitment), aggregates at line 507 (g1_lincomb_fast) where infinity contributes zero to weighted sum, but corresponding proof remains in proof_lincomb (line 903), creating pairing equation imbalance that allows forged cell data to pass. Severity calibration: c-kzg-4844 is a shared cryptographic library used by ALL Ethereum clients (Prysm, Lighthouse, Teku, Nimbus, Lodestar for CL; Geth, Nethermind, Besu, Erigon, Reth for EL). Single exploit affects entire network (100% > 50% threshold). Meets Critical criteria per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json: 'Take down the entire network by sending a single malicious on-chain transaction that ends up crashing all clients' (adapted for P2P vector). Bug bounty eligible (external-reachable, P2P entry point, attacker-controlled input).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: 01e_PARTIAL_MANUAL_kzg_infinity.json property PROP-57888860-inv-053: 'forall batch in verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch: forall i: commitments[i] != G1_IDENTITY, OR the aggregation must weight identity-point commitments such that verification still detects invalid proofs'
